---
title: "Modelling options for reporting delays"
subtitle: "InSight Net nowcasting strike team"
author: "Sam Abbott"
date: "2026-04-26"
format:
  revealjs:
    output: modelling-options-slides.html
    footer: "Modelling options for reporting delays"
    slide-level: 2
    chalkboard: true
    incremental: false
engine: knitr
---

## From three examples to a methods view

You have just seen three concrete cases:

- Massachusetts (respiratory ED visits via NSSP)
- Washington State (case investigation, COVID-19)
- South Carolina (measles)

Different surveillance streams, different operational responses, but the same underlying data picture.

::: {.fragment}
![](figures/truncation_final.png){fig-align="center" width="55%"}

Most events of interest have happened, but some are not yet visible to us — *right truncation*.

::: {.aside}
Figure from the [NFIDD short course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid/) (Abbott, Funk, Lison, Bracher, Bosse).
:::
:::

## Where models attach

Modelling for reporting delays is broader than nowcasting alone.

```{tikz}
%%| fig-align: center
%%| fig-ext: png
%%| fig-width: 9
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  every node/.style={align=center, font=\small},
  stage/.style={draw, rounded corners, minimum height=10mm,
                minimum width=28mm, fill=blue!8},
  method/.style={draw, dashed, rounded corners, minimum height=11mm,
                 minimum width=32mm, fill=yellow!18},
  arr/.style={->, thick, >=latex},
  marr/.style={->, dashed, gray, >=latex}
]
  \node[stage] (a) at (0,0) {Events occur};
  \node[stage] (b) at (3.7,0) {Events reported};
  \node[stage] (c) at (7.4,0) {Partial counts seen};
  \node[stage] (d) at (11.1,0) {Decisions};

  \draw[arr] (a) -- node[above, font=\scriptsize\itshape]{delay} (b);
  \draw[arr] (b) -- (c);
  \draw[arr] (c) -- (d);

  \node[method] (m1) at (0,-2.2) {Delay distribution\\estimation};
  \node[method] (m2) at (3.7,-2.2) {Modelling the\\reporting process};
  \node[method] (m3) at (7.4,-2.2) {Nowcasting\\(joint or two-step)};
  \node[method] (m4) at (11.1,-2.2) {Forecasting with\\reporting delays};

  \draw[marr] (m1) -- (a);
  \draw[marr] (m2) -- (b);
  \draw[marr] (m3) -- (c);
  \draw[marr] (m4) -- (d);
\end{tikzpicture}
```

Each one attaches to a different point in the same pipeline; the right choice depends on the question, not the method.

## Trade-offs at a glance

| Approach | Best when | Watch out for |
|---|---|---|
| Simple (pruning, caveats, report-date) | Delays short and stable; trends not levels | No uncertainty; throws away information |
| Multiplicative (chain ladder) | Stable delay pattern; moderate-to-large counts | Fixed-delay assumption; no day-of-week |
| Regression | Delays change over time; covariates matter | Delay not constrained; hard to add mechanism |
| Generative (Bayesian, mechanistic) | Need delay constraints, parametric delays, pooling, mechanism | More work to set up |
| Other statistical (ML, etc.) | Quick prototyping; large datasets | Hard to diagnose when wrong |

: {.striped .hover tbl-colwidths="[20,40,40]"}

More detail in the guide's [modelling options](../methods/index.qmd) section.

## What this means for the exercise

Four questions worth keeping in mind as you sketch your tree:

1. **What is the question?** A current level, a trend, a forecast, or a delay characterisation?
2. **What data do you have?** Snapshots, line list, full reporting triangle?
3. **How stable are the delays?** Fixed pattern, drifting, or changing fast?
4. **What capacity do you have?** Time, infrastructure, modelling experience, communication needs?

The decision tree exercise will work through how these questions narrow the choice.

## Our tools and courses

Most of the methods we work on sit in the [epinowcast](https://www.epinowcast.org/) community:

- [epinowcast](https://package.epinowcast.org/), [epidist](https://epidist.epinowcast.org/), [primarycensored](https://primarycensored.epinowcast.org/), [baselinenowcast](https://baselinenowcast.epinowcast.org/) — R packages for nowcasting, delay estimation, and primary event censored distributions
- [EpiNow2](https://epiforecasts.io/EpiNow2/) — R package for Bayesian Rt and forecasting with reporting delays
- The [EpiAware](https://github.com/EpiAware) org for the same ideas in Julia, including [CensoredDistributions.jl](https://github.com/EpiAware/CensoredDistributions.jl)

We also teach a free open-source short course on these topics:

- [NFIDD](https://nfidd.github.io/nfidd/) — *Nowcasting and forecasting of infectious disease dynamics*, available online any time
- Taught in person at [SISMID 2026](https://sph.emory.edu/SISMID/) (Emory, July 2026): [course site](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid/)

I have kept this overview generic on purpose.
If any of these are relevant to your setting, please grab me after the session.

## Discussion

Questions, then on to the next section.

[Back to the session overview](index.qmd)
